meta.subtitle: Plain-English market read · Gap-risk check · Iran talks continue as oil rolls over

verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size / A-grade only
verdict.desc: The tape has calmed sharply. Oil is rolling over — WTI slid back toward ~$72 on a surprise U.S. inventory build and reports that U.S.–Iran peace talks will continue despite the week’s strikes. The VIX fell back to ~15.3, the S&P edged to a fresh weekly high near 7,570, and Delta beat earnings and rose. Gap risk eased to 44/100 (MILD) and P(≥1% gap) dropped to 31% — back below the 35% trigger. But this is a Friday into a live-conflict weekend with CPI Tue 7/14 and PPI Wed 7/15 just ahead, so any weekend Iran headline is an untradeable gap. Half size, A-grade only; keep every spread short-dated so it clears both the weekend and next week’s inflation prints.

composite.band_note: MILD · down from 64 · oil rolls over, talks continue

gap.heading: P(≥1% Overnight Gap to Mon Open)
gap.band_note: BELOW the 35% line · but a full weekend of headline risk

focus.jbig: Oil rolls over · talks continue · tape calms
focus.jtag: US–Iran negotiations resume, oil slides, VIX back below 16 — but weekend gap risk remains
focus.jnote: The environment improved this week — the war premium is bleeding out. WTI slid back near $72, down about 2% today, after the EIA reported a surprise ~3M-barrel crude inventory build (the first since April) and reports that Washington and Tehran will keep peace talks going despite two days of strikes. President Trump still calls the ceasefire “over,” but noted negotiations continue and a Qatari delegation reached Iran. Equities notched a winning week — the S&P edged up to ~7,570, an AI-led Nasdaq is firm, and the VIX fell back to ~15.3, its lowest in a month. Gold steadied near $4,110 and the 10-year eased to ~4.54%. It’s calmer — but it is a Friday into a live-conflict weekend, so a Sniper trims size rather than pressing.
focus.notdown: Delta (DAL) is the standout: it beat with $1.56 adj. EPS vs ~$1.48 expected on record revenue, affirmed full-year guidance and raised its dividend — the stock rose. That is a clean fresh post-earnings gap-up (Type A) with earnings risk now behind it — the one genuinely tradeable Sniper setup today. Energy still catches a bid on any oil headline but is now a fading war premium, not a clean lean.
focus.notup: Airlines-as-a-short is off the table — Delta’s beat-and-raise removes the bear-call thesis for the group. The fuel-cost worry that pressured airlines all week just got answered by pricing power. Fading strength into a de-escalating tape with a solid print is exactly the wrong side.
focus.why: Why YELLOW (not GREEN)? Composite fell to 44 (MILD) and P(≥1% gap) dropped to 31%, back under the 35% trigger — so the hard lockouts have cleared. But two things keep it from GREEN: it’s a Friday into a live U.S.–Iran weekend where any strike headline gaps Monday’s open, and CPI (Tue 7/14) and PPI (Wed 7/15) plus the big-bank prints land next week. Trade half size, A-grade only, and keep DTE tight so spreads clear both the weekend and the inflation data.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~15.3, down ~3% and back to a one-month low as the war premium fades
Big events (next 1–3 days) — DAL beat & rose; CPI Tue 7/14, PPI Wed 7/15, big banks 7/14–15; live-conflict weekend
World risk — U.S.–Iran talks continue; ceasefire “over” but negotiations open, strikes paused
Volatility trend — front-month VIX falling faster than 3-month; curve normalizing away from stress
Commodities — oil rolling over, −2% on inventory build; premium unwinding, still the swing factor

snapshot.delta: beat & raised guide · rose on print

setups.read: The cleanest setup today. Delta beat with $1.56 adj. EPS vs ~$1.48 on record ~$17.7B revenue, affirmed full-year guidance of $6.50–$7.50, raised its dividend 15%, and guided Q3 above the Street — and the stock rose. Earnings risk is now behind it, and CEO Bastian says fare strength is sustainable even as fuel eases. A textbook Type A gap-up. Still confirm the move holds at the open (4-VAL) before selling a bull put beneath the day’s support; this is a headline-sensitive tape into a weekend.
setups.check_note: Run 4-VAL at 9:35 Mon: above prior close, above open, upper third of range. Half size — YELLOW day, weekend gap risk. Short strike below the post-earnings base.

setups.read: Energy still trades on oil headlines, but the tide turned: WTI slid back toward $72 on a surprise inventory build and de-escalation talks, so the bid is fading, not building. Selling a bull put here is now betting the war premium returns — the wrong side of a de-escalating tape. This was a two-sided war trade all week; today it’s simply losing its catalyst. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: Catalyst is unwinding, not building. No edge selling premium as the oil premium bleeds out. Stand aside.

setups.read: The defense bid was conflict-demand; with talks resuming and the strikes paused, that thesis is cooling. A peace headline gaps this group down. Selling a bull put here is still a bet the war continues — not the probability edge a Sniper wants, especially as the tape calms. Confirm no earnings inside DTE (LMT/RTX report late-Jul).
setups.check_note: War premium fading. Revisit only if a clean technical setup forms once the geopolitics settle.

setups.read: The tape is bid and the S&P is at a fresh weekly high, but the index sits just under its ~7,621 all-time high, not breaking out on heavy volume today. AI/chip names (Micron, Meta) ran hard this week but are extended, not fresh high-volume breakouts holding a clean pivot. Nothing qualifies as a textbook Type C into a weekend of Iran risk.
setups.check_note: No clean high-volume ATH breakout holding its pivot — nothing to validate today.

setups.read: No clean, durable single-stock news re-rate this morning outside the fading Iran/oil complex. SK Hynix’s Nasdaq debut popped ~14% but a fresh IPO with no options history and no support base is not a Sniper vehicle. Nothing else qualifies as a durable Type B.
setups.check_note: No qualifying durable news gap — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Calm drift, war premium fades
scenarios.sub: Talks continue, oil keeps easing, VIX stays low; equities grind up into next week’s CPI/bank prints

scenarios.label: Good case — Formal de-escalation
scenarios.sub: A concrete ceasefire/deal headline; oil gives back more of the spike, a relief bounce, vol compresses further

scenarios.label: Bad case — Weekend re-escalation
scenarios.sub: New strikes or a Gulf-base hit over the weekend; oil re-spikes, equities gap down Monday

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Weekend Iran headline risk — talks are continuing but the ceasefire is officially “over” and strikes could resume at any time. A weekend flare-up gaps Monday’s open before you can react — the number-one reason to trim size today.
Strait of Hormuz shipping — roughly a fifth of the world’s oil moves through it and transit is still below normal. Any renewed attack or closure sends crude (and the war premium) straight back up.

CPI · Tue 7/14, PPI · Wed 7/15 — the week’s macro pivot. Markets still price at least one Fed hike in 2026; a hot inflation print on lingering oil stickiness would jolt rates and stocks. Keep DTE clear of it.
Big banks · ~7/14–15 — JPM, BAC, C, WFC open the real Q2 season the same days as CPI/PPI, stacking single-stock and macro risk. Any financials spread must expire before its print.

Oil round-trip — WTI has round-tripped from ~$68 to ~$74 and back toward $72 in a week; the inventory build helped today but the swing factor can reverse on one headline. It’s still the market’s dominant lever.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is now underway — Delta reported this morning with a clean beat-and-raise (EPS $1.56 vs ~$1.48, stock up), following PepsiCo’s slight miss Thursday. The banks land next week alongside CPI/PPI. With the Iran conflict cooling but not resolved, keep every spread clear of its name’s print and clear of next week’s inflation data.

rows.why: Reported adj. EPS $1.56 vs ~$1.48 on record ~$17.7B revenue (up ~14%), affirmed FY guide $6.50–$7.50, guided Q3 above Street, and raised the dividend 15%. Premium seat revenue topped main cabin for the first time. Even with record fuel costs, pricing power carried the quarter.
rows.angle: This is the day’s one clean Type A. A bull put below the post-earnings base is valid at half size once 4-VAL confirms Monday — earnings risk is behind it.

rows.why: Adj. EPS $2.20 vs $2.21 consensus — a one-cent miss — and the stock slid as much as 3.3% before paring to ~−1.7%. A soft print in a risk-off week, not a clean gap either way. Already old news by Monday.
rows.angle: No clean Type A — a mild-miss fade isn’t a Sniper setup. Move on.

rows.why: The real season opener — and they land the same days as CPI (7/14) and PPI (7/15), stacking macro and single-stock risk. Any bank spread must expire before its print, and mind the inflation overlap.
rows.angle: Keep any financials spread to 7 days or fewer so it clears the earnings date — and stay light into CPI/PPI.

earnings.outro: Fri 7/10: Delta’s beat-and-raise gives the season a firm open after PepsiCo’s soft print. It gets real next week: big banks ~Tue–Wed 7/14–15 alongside CPI/PPI, then the first mega-caps the week after — all against a cooling-but-live Iran backdrop.

rows.delta: upgraded — tape calms, gap tail smaller
